title: Dryopteris loheriana
intro: Dryopteris loheriana, also known as Loher's wood fern, is a species of fern native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its lance-shaped fronds and unique habit of growing on trees. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris loheriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Loher's wood fern is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve pain, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Dryopteris loheriana is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a member of the genus Dryopteris, which includes over 250 species of ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns or autumn ferns. The genus Dryopteris is part of the larger order Polypodiales, which includes many other species of ferns. The classification of Dryopteris loheriana is a specific example of the more general classification of the plant kingdom.
